hook.text: Cool certain metals enough and electricity flows forever with zero resistance. Quantum mechanics makes this possible.

buildup.text: In 1911, Heike Kamerlingh Onnes cooled mercury to near absolute zero and resistance vanished completely.

discovery.text: Electrons pair up into Cooper pairs that glide through the metal without scattering. Zero energy loss.

twist.text: Superfluids are even stranger—liquid helium near absolute zero climbs walls and flows through tiny pores.

climax.text: MRI machines, particle accelerators, and maglev trains all rely on superconducting magnets.

punchline.text: Zero resistance. Infinite flow. Quantum cold is powerful.
